counters.description: ashe is annoying to play against because her poke with her w combined with the slow make your lane unplayable. if you manage to hit her with your q she has no mobility either so she can be caught out if she miss positions.
brand is a champion with a lot of damage, but you can also block a lot of this damage with your e - unbreakable. when he ult try to catch it with your e to make his ult disappear in thin air.

karma will definitely poke you out of lane, so be careful for that. when you manage to stay healthy in lane, you can try to engage. karma  will be able to escape using her w or e, so a hard matchup overall.

lulu will only do one thing in this lane and that is polymorph either you or your adc when you engage. you can block the polymorph with your e as this is considered a projectile. furthermore she will poke you down and you can’t do a lot about it so try to stay healthy in lane!

lux does a lot of damage, she will poke you down just like karma. however, if you get on top of her, this will result in a kill as she has no mobility besides flash. you can block her q with your e, but be careful you will get rooted!

nami has some fun interactions with you e as you can catch bubbles mid-air and make her ultimate disappear. nami has a lot of sustain and poke in laning phase so that will make it hard to play lane, but you will have her number in teamfights.

rakan is a very mobile champion, which makes it hard to catch him. when you engage on his carry he will immediately follow and counter engage on your carry. try to survive in this lane as he will have an easy time against you, because punishing him is hard as braum

senna has a lot of range to work with, which makes laning very difficult. she has a lot of damage and sustain as well, so try to neutralise this lane the best you can. in teamfights you can block a lot of her damage with your e.

shaco has boxes which fear you and that makes it really annoying to play against him. when looking for bush control or engages you will definetely step into one, so an early oracle lens can help a lot.
soraka is a difficult matchup as she is good at sustaining and her q will slow you, which makes your vulnerable. highly adviced to buy anti-heal.

swain is a "support" with a lot of damage, crowd control en healing. when playing against him, getting anti-healing is a near must for either you or your adc. for teamfights: if swain  ults you can slow him with q or r to create distance and let his ult run out. after that you can freely re-engage.
velkoz is another of those "supports" that does a lot of damage. just try to survive lane by staying healthy and punish him for his cooldowns.

xerath is also a "support" with damage and crowd control. stay healthy in lane and play around his cooldowns, especially his e which is pretty long. when you get on top of him, you can burst him down quickly with the help of your adc. you might have trouble blocking his damage as you can’t block his w and r.

zilean is annoying for the fact that he has a 99% on click slow in the later stages of the game and a revive with a low cooldown. you can catch bombs mid-air, but be careful as you will get stunned when he tosses two on your head.
zyra her plants can be annoying and she has some steady poke with her q. play around her e and punish her when she misplaces her plants or e.

synergies.description: aphelios is a champion that excels when peeled correctly. that's why it's a good pairing with braum.
an oppressive combo with a lot of slows. ashe's slows set up for an easy hit of your q and a slow.
caitlyn's long range help with stacking your passive faster, but her long range also makes her play way back from where you want to position.
peeling draven makes braum an okay pairing, but the agressive playstyle is something braum has a hard time keeping up with.
ezreal and braum make a strong pairing as ezreal's q will work for braum passive aswell.
jhin has only 4 auto attacks before he needs to reload and his attack speed is super low. braum doesn't go well with jhin.
good peeling for a jinx and some lockdown to take someone down quick. fun duo to play!
kaisa can stack braum's passive relatively quick so a strong pairing.
once you stack your passive, kalista gets to free hit and put some extra spears in an enemy. strong combo!
kog'maw needs some good peeling to excel. he is quick at stacking your passive aswell, so late game a super strong combo.
lucian stacks your passive super quick with his passive. might be the strongest combo of them all.
miss fortune's ult and your ult setup for a good wombo combo.
with nilah's short range, double ranged matchups can be very painful. make sure to not go double short range into double long range!
samira has a more agressive playstyle and can be hard to keep up and set her up.
senna's q also helps stacking your passive, so that will stack pretty quickly. besides that senna can help sustain you in lane.
sivir needs to scale and by neutralising a lane, she can gets the time to scale. keep her safe and you have a late game guarantee.

smolder enjoys some strong peeling to survive. the door will help you achieve that. when you manage to go late and you can keep smolder going in teamfights you have a high chance of winning your games!
tristana's agressive playstyle will be difficult to keep up with. if she jumps forward you can follow up with your w and kick off the fight.
twitch has a lot of teamfight strengths, but laning phase will be hard without a lot of pressure.
this synergy depends on the build varus goes. if it's on-hit varus you have a strong duo, but with poke varus it's super weak.
peel your vayne early game and let her get farm. if she hits 3 items you are good to go.
xayah's feather setup help a lot with locking down a target. makes a strong pairing, but rakan is better with her! :p

zeri is a champ that wants to go forward a lot and you have a hard time to follow up. kiting backwards and peeling for zeri on the other hand is really strong. this duo works in some matchups but isn't the best to go for.
aphelios is a champion that excels when peeled correctly. that's why it's a good pairing with braum.
an oppressive combo with a lot of slows. ashe's slows set up for an easy hit of your q and a slow.
